steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The transition must link the need for standards to the development of the SI system.

The first sentence establishes a principle (precise standards ensure validity), while the second introduces the solution (SI units based on natural values).

The strongest clue is the cause-and-effect relationship between the first sentence's claim and the second's explanation of implementation.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice D, "For this reason," directly pairs with the cause-and-effect logic: scientists rely on precise standards (cause), so metrologists developed the SI system (effect).

The other three choices either introduce a contrast (A), disregard context (B), or imply additional unrelated information (C).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
